## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE OVERVIEW

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE AND ORGANIZATION

```

```

### 2. TECH STACK

**Frontend:**
- **Framework:** Next.js 16.1.6 (React 19.2.3)
- **Language:** TypeScript 5
- **UI Components:** Shadcn/ui, Radix UI
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4, PostCSS
- **Icons:** Lucide React
- **Drag & Drop:** @dnd-kit (core, sortable, utilities)
- **Carousel:** Embla Carousel React
- **Date Handling:** date-fns 4.1.0, react-day-picker
- **Toast Notifications:** Sonner
- **Theme:** next-themes
- **State Management:** React Context API (Authentication)
- **Utility:** clsx, tailwind-merge

**No Backend:**
- Pure frontend application with client-side data storage
- In-memory data structures in TypeScript files
- localStorage for authentication

### 3. MAIN FEATURES AND MODULES

#### **Core Features:**
1. **Authentication & Authorization**
   - Role-based access control (5 roles)
   - localStorage-based session management
   - Quick login feature for demo users

2. **Project Management**
   - Create, read, update projects
   - Project status tracking (planning, in_progress, review, on_hold, completed, cancelled)
   - Priority levels (low, medium, high, urgent)
   - Team member assignment
   - Client association
   - Budget tracking

3. **Task Management**
   - Task creation and assignment
   - Task status workflow (todo, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked)
   - Time tracking (estimated vs. logged hours)
   - Priority and due date management
   - Milestone linking
   - Tags support

4. **Milestone Tracking**
   - Milestone creation and management
   - Status tracking (pending, in_progress, completed, overdue)
   - Project-level milestone sequencing
   - Due date management

5. **Budget Management**
   - Budget request creation with line items
   - Dual-approval workflow (agency + client)
   - Budget status tracking (draft, pending, agency_approved, client_approved, approved, rejected, revised)
   - Category-based breakdown (design, development, production, strategy, materials, software, other)
   - Historical tracking with approval notes

6. **Project Briefs**
   - Multi-version brief management
   - Versioning system with change notes
   - Brief status workflow (draft, pending_approval, approved, revision_requested, rejected)
   - Attachment system with metadata (documents, images, audio, video, files)
   - Attachment source tracking (email, call, message, meeting, direct, other)

7. **Deliverables**
   - Deliverable tracking with versioning
   - Status workflow (in_progress, in_review, approved, revision_requested)
   - Version history with upload metadata
   - Approval tracking

8. **Expenses**
   - Expense recording and categorization
   - Status tracking (submitted, verified, disputed)
   - Category-based breakdown
   - Project-level cost tracking

9. **Time Tracking**
   - Timesheet management
   - Status workflow (draft, submitted, approved, rejected)
   - Time entry records

10. **Dashboard & Reporting**
    - Role-specific dashboards
    - Activity logs
    - Project progress visualization
    - Task overview
    - Milestone tracking
    - Budget summaries

### 4. KEY FILES AND PURPOSES

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/frontend/app/(app)/page.tsx` | Role-aware dashboard home page |
| `/frontend/app/(app)/layout.tsx` | App layout with sidebar navigation |
| `/frontend/app/auth/signin/page.tsx` | Login page with demo user buttons |
| `/frontend/lib/auth.tsx` | AuthContext provider & useAuth hook |
| `/frontend/lib/utils.ts` | Utility functions (currency, color mappings) |
| `/frontend/data/users.ts` | User data model & authentication lookup |
| `/frontend/data/projects.ts` | Project CRUD + role-based filtering |
| `/frontend/data/tasks.ts` | Task CRUD + assignment & filtering |
| `/frontend/data/milestones.ts` | Milestone CRUD & project association |
| `/frontend/data/budget-requests.ts` | Budget workflow & approval logic |
| `/frontend/data/briefs.ts` | Brief versioning & attachment handling |
| `/frontend/components/app-sidebar.tsx` | Role-based navigation sidebar |
| `/frontend/components/projects/kanban-board.tsx` | Drag-drop task kanban view |

### 5. API ENDPOINTS AND ROUTES

**Note:** This is a client-side only application with no backend API. All data is managed client-side.

**Public Routes:**
- `GET /auth/signin` — Sign-in page

**Authenticated Routes (Protected by `/app/(app)/layout.tsx`):**
- `GET /` — Home dashboard (role-aware)
- `GET /projects` — Projects list
- `GET /projects/new` — Create project (PM/Manager)
- `GET /projects/[id]` — Project detail view
- `GET /projects/[id]/edit` — Edit project (PM only)
- `GET /projects/[id]/tasks/kanban` — Kanban board for project tasks
- `GET /projects/[id]/brief/[briefId]` — Project brief detail
- `GET /projects/[id]/brief/[briefId]/edit` — Edit brief version

- `GET /tasks` — All tasks list
- `GET /tasks/new` — Create task
- `GET /tasks/[id]` — Task detail
- `GET /tasks/[id]/edit` — Edit task

- `GET /milestones` — Milestones list
- `GET /milestones/new` — Create milestone (Manager)
- `GET /milestones/[id]` — Milestone detail

- `GET /budgets` — Budgets list
- `GET /budgets/new` — Create budget request (PM/Manager)
- `GET /budgets/[id]` — Budget detail

### 6. DATA MODELS/SCHEMAS

**User Model:**
```typescript
interface User {
  id: string;
  email: string;
  password: string;
  name: string;
  role: "agency_owner" | "ops_admin" | "project_manager" | "client" | "team_member";
  avatar?: string;
}
```

**Project Model:**
```typescript
interface Project {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  clientId: string;
  projectManagerId: string;
  teamMemberIds: string[];
  status: ProjectStatus;
  priority: ProjectPriority;
  startDate: string;
  endDate: string;
  totalBudget: number;
  currency: string;
  tags: string[];
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

**Task Model:**
```typescript
interface Task {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  milestoneId: string;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  assignedTo: string;
  status: "todo" | "in_progress" | "in_review" | "done" | "blocked";
  priority: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "urgent";
  estimatedHours: number;
  loggedHours: number;
  dueDate: string;
  tags: string[];
}
```

**Milestone Model:**
```typescript
interface Milestone {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  dueDate: string;
  status: "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed" | "overdue";
  order: number;
}
```

**Budget Request Model:**
```typescript
interface BudgetRequest {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  requestedBy: string;
  status: BudgetStatus;
  lineItems: BudgetLineItem[];
  totalAmount: number;
  submittedAt: string;
  agencyApprovedBy?: string;
  agencyApprovedAt?: string;
  clientApprovedBy?: string;
  clientApprovedAt?: string;
  notes?: string;
}
```

**Brief Model (with versioning):**
```typescript
interface Brief {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  currentVersion: number;
  versions: BriefVersion[];
  status: "draft" | "pending_approval" | "approved" | "revision_requested" | "rejected";
  submittedBy?: string;
  approvedBy?: string;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

**Deliverable Model:**
```typescript
interface Deliverable {
  id: string;
  projectId: string;
  milestoneId: string;
  taskId: string;
  title: string;
  status: "in_progress" | "in_review" | "approved" | "revision_requested";
  currentVersion: string;
  versions: DeliverableVersion[];
  approvedBy?: string;
  approvedAt?: string;
}
```

### 7. AUTHENTICATION/AUTHORIZATION APPROACH

**Authentication:**
- Client-side authentication via React Context API
- localStorage-based session persistence (key: `auth_user`)
- Simple email/password validation against hardcoded users in data/users.ts
- No password hashing or encryption (demo application)

**Authorization:**
- Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with 5 roles:
  - `agency_owner` — Full access to all data
  - `ops_admin` — Administrative access to operations
  - `project_manager` — Project and team management
  - `team_member` — Limited access (assigned tasks/projects)
  - `client` — Read-only access to own projects

**Role-Based Features:**
- Navigation items show/hide based on role
- Dashboard displays role-specific data
- Budget approval workflows differentiate agency vs. client approvals
- Project filtering based on user role and associations

**Auth Context Hook:**
```typescript
useAuth(): {
  user: AuthUser | null;
  login: (email: string, password: string) => boolean;
  logout: () => void;
  isLoading: boolean;
}
```

### 8. CONFIGURATION FILES

**package.json:**
- Next.js 16.1.6, React 19.2.3
- Tailwind CSS 4, TypeScript 5
- Radix UI, Shadcn/ui components
- Drag-drop (@dnd-kit), date utilities
- ESLint configuration

**tsconfig.json:**
- ES2017 target, strict mode enabled
- Path alias: `@/*` pointing to root
- Isolated modules, incremental compilation

**next.config.ts:**
- Minimal configuration
- Default Next.js build settings

**tailwind.config.ts:**
- Tailwind CSS with Shadcn/ui preset
- CSS variable-based color system
- Status colors for different entity types

**components.json:**
- Shadcn/ui configuration
- TypeScript support
- Tailwind CSS integration

### 9. DEMO DATA

The application includes 4 complete projects with realistic data:

1. **NovaTech Dashboard Redesign** (prj_001)
   - Status: In Progress
   - Budget: ₹37.5 lakhs
   - 4 milestones, 9+ tasks
   - Approved brief (2 versions)
   - Multiple deliverables in review

2. **Lumière Spring Campaign** (prj_002)
   - Status: In Review
   - Budget: ₹60 lakhs
   - Video & photography production
   - Brief pending approval

3. **FoodieApp Mobile Design** (prj_003)
   - Status: In Progress
   - Budget: ₹23.3 lakhs
   - Mobile app UI design
   - Draft brief

4. **FitPulse App Launch** (prj_004)
   - Status: Planning
   - Budget: ₹46.7 lakhs
   - Full product launch campaign
   - 5 milestones planned

**Test Users (all use password "123456"):**
- owner@agency.com — Agency Owner
- ops@agency.com — Ops Admin
- pm@agency.com, pm2@agency.com — Project Managers
- team@agency.com, dev@agency.com, copy@agency.com, anim@agency.com — Team Members
- client@agency.com, client2@agency.com — Clients

### 10. KEY FEATURES HIGHLIGHTS

- **Role-Based Dashboards:** Different view for each user role (owner sees all, team member sees assigned tasks)
- **Project Progress Tracking:** Visual progress bars based on task completion
- **Kanban Board:** Drag-and-drop task management for projects
- **Dual-Approval Budget Workflow:** Agency and client must both approve budgets
- **Brief Versioning:** Track brief changes with detailed version history
- **Deliverable Versioning:** Multiple version uploads with approval workflow
- **Attachment Management:** Briefs support multiple attachment types with source tracking
- **Expense Tracking:** Project-level expense categorization and verification
- **Time Tracking:** Estimated vs. logged hours for tasks
- **Tag System:** Tags for projects, tasks, and other entities
- **Currency Formatting:** Indian Rupee (INR) formatting throughout
- **Dark Mode:** Application built with dark theme support (Shadcn/ui)
- **Responsive Design:** Mobile-friendly UI with Tailwind CSS

### SUMMARY

This is a **full-featured Project Management Dashboard** built with Next.js and TypeScript. It's a **client-side only application** (no backend) designed for managing creative agency projects with complex workflows involving budgets, deliverables, briefs, and multi-party approvals. The application demonstrates modern React patterns, role-based access control, and comprehensive UI component design using Shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS.

   410→export function getBriefByProject(projectId: string): Brief | undefined {
   411→  return briefs.find((b) => b.projectId === projectId);
   412→}
   413→
   414→export function getBriefById(id: string): Brief | undefined {
   415→  return briefs.find((b) => b.id === id);
   416→}
   417→
   418→export function getLatestBriefVersion(brief: Brief): BriefVersion {
   419→  const version = brief.versions.find(
   420→    (v) => v.version === brief.currentVersion
   421→  );
   422→  if (!version) {
   423→    throw new Error(
   424→      `Current version ${brief.currentVersion} not found in brief ${brief.id}`
   425→    );
   426→  }
   427→  return version;
   428→}
   429→
   430→export function updateBriefVersion(
   431→  id: string,
   432→  data: Partial<BriefVersion>
   433→): Brief | undefined {
   434→  const briefIndex = briefs.findIndex((b) => b.id === id);
   435→  if (briefIndex === -1) return undefined;
   436→  const brief = briefs[briefIndex];
   437→  const versionIndex = brief.versions.findIndex(
   438→    (v) => v.version === brief.currentVersion
   439→  );
   440→  if (versionIndex === -1) return undefined;
   441→  brief.versions[versionIndex] = {
   442→    ...brief.versions[versionIndex],
   443→    ...data,
   444→    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   445→  };
   446→  brief.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   447→  return brief;
   448→}

Brief Approval Workflow UI #49
Open
@Vanshbordia
Description
Vanshbordia
opened 1 hour ago
Files: frontend/data/briefs.ts, frontend/components/projects/brief-detail.tsx

Add mutation functions and UI buttons for the brief approval workflow: PM submits for approval, client approves/rejects/requests revision. When approved, the brief must be locked (no further edits).

Context:

Brief has status, submittedBy?, submittedAt?, approvedBy?, approvedAt? fields
brief-detail.tsx already renders status badges and approval info — add action buttons to this component
useAuth() from @/lib/auth provides user with role field
Roles: "project_manager" can submit; "client" can approve/reject/request revision
Tasks:


Add submitBriefForApproval(id: string, userId: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "pending_approval", submittedBy = userId, submittedAt = new Date().toISOString()

Add approveBrief(id: string, userId: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "approved", approvedBy = userId, approvedAt = new Date().toISOString()

Add requestRevision(id: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "revision_requested"

Add rejectBrief(id: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "rejected"

In frontend/components/projects/brief-detail.tsx, add a "Submit for Approval" <Button> — visible when user.role === "project_manager" and brief.status === "draft". On click, call submitBriefForApproval() and update local state

In the same component, add "Approve", "Request Revision", "Reject" <Button>s — visible when user.role === "client" and brief.status === "pending_approval". On click, call the corresponding function and update local state

When brief.status === "approved", show a <Lock> icon next to the status badge and disable all edit links/buttons

Brief Approval Workflow UI #49
Open
@Vanshbordia
Description
Vanshbordia
opened 1 hour ago
Files: frontend/data/briefs.ts, frontend/components/projects/brief-detail.tsx

Add mutation functions and UI buttons for the brief approval workflow: PM submits for approval, client approves/rejects/requests revision. When approved, the brief must be locked (no further edits).

Context:

Brief has status, submittedBy?, submittedAt?, approvedBy?, approvedAt? fields
brief-detail.tsx already renders status badges and approval info — add action buttons to this component
useAuth() from @/lib/auth provides user with role field
Roles: "project_manager" can submit; "client" can approve/reject/request revision
Tasks:


Add submitBriefForApproval(id: string, userId: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "pending_approval", submittedBy = userId, submittedAt = new Date().toISOString()

Add approveBrief(id: string, userId: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "approved", approvedBy = userId, approvedAt = new Date().toISOString()

Add requestRevision(id: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "revision_requested"

Add rejectBrief(id: string) to frontend/data/briefs.ts — sets status = "rejected"

In frontend/components/projects/brief-detail.tsx, add a "Submit for Approval" <Button> — visible when user.role === "project_manager" and brief.status === "draft". On click, call submitBriefForApproval() and update local state

In the same component, add "Approve", "Request Revision", "Reject" <Button>s — visible when user.role === "client" and brief.status === "pending_approval". On click, call the corresponding function and update local state

When brief.status === "approved", show a <Lock> icon next to the status badge and disable all edit links/buttons

However, there's an issue: calling hooks (`useState`) after other hooks but conditionally after `useEffect` calls is fine. But `useState` should ideally be called before `useEffect`. In React, hooks must be called in the same order. Looking at the code:

Actually, I'm realizing the real issue here: `useState` is being called after `useEffect`, which violates the rules of hooks. Hooks must always be called in the same order on every render, and they can't be called conditionally or between other hooks. The code structure has `useState` calls interspersed with `useEffect` calls, which breaks this rule.

Wait, let me reconsider. Looking at the actual code flow, all these hooks are being called at the top level without any conditionals before them. The order is consistent across renders: `useState` for activeVersion, then `useEffect` for auth, then `useState` for brief, then `useEffect` for activeVersion setup, then `useEffect` for document.title. This is actually fine from React's perspective since the order never changes.

Here's a summary of all changes:

**[data/briefs.ts](frontend/data/briefs.ts)** — 4 new mutation functions:
- `submitBriefForApproval(id, userId)` → sets `status: "pending_approval"`, `submittedBy`, `submittedAt`
- `approveBrief(id, userId)` → sets `status: "approved"`, `approvedBy`, `approvedAt`
- `requestRevision(id)` → sets `status: "revision_requested"`
- `rejectBrief(id)` → sets `status: "rejected"`

**[components/projects/brief-detail.tsx](frontend/components/projects/brief-detail.tsx)**:
- Added `useState<Brief>` initialized from the `brief` prop to track local state
- "Submit for Approval" button → calls `submitBriefForApproval()`, updates state (visible to PM when draft/revision_requested)
- "Approve", "Reject", "Request Revision" buttons → call corresponding functions, update state (visible to client when pending_approval)
- Lock icon + banner already existed for approved status

**[components/projects/brief-version-page.tsx](frontend/components/projects/brief-version-page.tsx)**:
- Same pattern: `useState<Brief>` for local state, 4 handler functions
- All 4 action buttons wired up with `onClick`
- "Edit Brief" link is guarded by `!isApproved` — hidden when brief is locked
